2848.iwh_en: It was narrated from Khalid ibn Ma'dan that Abdullah ibn al-Ahtam entered upon Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz and spoke about the Sunnah of the Prophet ﷺ. Umar wept until his tears fell on his cheeks. Then he said: I have heard that the Sunnah will leave the earth just as a shooting star leaves the sky.
2848.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Khalid ibn Ma'dan bahwa Abdullah ibn al-Ahtam masuk menemui Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz dan berbicara tentang Sunnah Nabi ﷺ. Umar menangis hingga air matanya jatuh di pipinya. Kemudian ia berkata: Aku telah mendengar bahwa Sunnah akan meninggalkan bumi sebagaimana bintang jatuh meninggalkan langit.

2849.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Jawza' Aws ibn Abdullah that he said: 'The people of Madinah were afflicted by a severe drought. They complained to Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her). She said: Look at the grave of the Prophet ﷺ and open a window toward the sky so there is no roof between it and the sky. He said: They did so and were given such rain that the vegetation grew and the camels became fat from it — so much so that that year was called the year of abundance.'
2849.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Jawza' Aws ibn Abdullah bahwa ia berkata: 'Penduduk Madinah ditimpa kekeringan yang parah. Mereka mengadu kepada Aisha (radhiyallahu 'anha). Ia berkata: Lihatlah kubur Nabi ﷺ dan bukalah sebuah jendela ke arah langit sehingga tidak ada atap antara kubur dan langit. Ia berkata: Mereka melakukannya dan hujan diturunkan sehingga tanaman tumbuh dan unta menjadi gemuk karenanya — begitu banyak sehingga tahun itu disebut tahun kelimpahan.'

2850.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sa'id ibn Abd al-Aziz that he said: 'During the days of al-Harrah, the adhan was not called in the mosque of the Prophet ﷺ for three days, nor was the iqamah. Said ibn al-Musayyib did not leave the mosque. He did not know the time of prayer except by the sounds he heard from the direction of the Prophet's grave ﷺ.'
2850.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sa'id ibn Abd al-Aziz bahwa ia berkata: 'Pada hari-hari al-Harrah, adzan tidak dikumandangkan di masjid Nabi ﷺ selama tiga hari, demikian pula iqamah. Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib tidak meninggalkan masjid. Ia tidak mengetahui waktu shalat kecuali dari suara-suara yang ia dengar dari arah kubur Nabi ﷺ.'

2851.iwh_en: It was narrated from Nubayh ibn Wahb that Ka'b entered upon Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her), and they mentioned the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Ka'b said: 'There is no day that passes at dawn except that seventy thousand angels descend and encircle the grave of the Prophet ﷺ, striking their wings, and send blessings upon the Prophet until the evening comes. Then when evening comes, they ascend and seventy thousand others descend and do the same, until when the earth splits open he will emerge with the greatest army that has ever walked the earth.'
2851.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Nubayh ibn Wahb bahwa Ka'b masuk menemui Aisha (radhiyallahu 'anha), dan mereka menyebut Rasulullah ﷺ. Ka'b berkata: 'Tidak ada satu hari pun yang berlalu di waktu fajar kecuali tujuh puluh ribu malaikat turun dan mengelilingi kubur Nabi ﷺ, mengepakkan sayap mereka, dan bershalawat kepada Nabi hingga sore hari tiba. Kemudian ketika sore hari tiba, mereka naik dan tujuh puluh ribu yang lain turun dan melakukan hal yang sama, hingga ketika bumi terbelah beliau akan keluar bersama pasukan terbesar yang pernah berjalan di bumi.'

2852.iwh_en: It was narrated from Irbad ibn Sariyah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ led us in the Fajr prayer, then gave us a sermon that caused our eyes to weep and our hearts to tremble. A man said: O Messenger of Allah, this is as if it were a farewell sermon — so what do you advise us? He said: I advise you to fear Allah and to hear and obey, even if an Abyssinian slave is placed over you. Indeed whoever of you lives will see many differences — so hold fast to my Sunnah and the Sunnah of the rightly-guided caliphs who come after me. Cling to it and hold on to it with your molars. And beware of newly invented matters, for every newly invented matter is an innovation, and every innovation is misguidance.'
2852.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Irbad ibn Sariyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ mengimami kami shalat Subuh, kemudian berkhutbah kepada kami dengan khutbah yang membuat mata kami menangis dan hati kami berguncang. Seorang pria berkata: Ya Rasulullah, ini seolah-olah adalah khutbah perpisahan — maka apa nasihatmu kepada kami? Beliau bersabda: Aku menasihatkan kalian untuk bertakwa kepada Allah dan mendengar serta taat, bahkan jika seorang budak Habsyi ditempatkan atas kalian. Sesungguhnya siapapun di antara kalian yang masih hidup akan melihat banyak perbedaan — maka berpeganglah teguh pada Sunnahku dan Sunnah para khalifah yang mendapat petunjuk setelahku. Peganglah itu dan gigitlah dengan gigi geraham kalian. Dan waspadalah terhadap perkara-perkara yang baru dibuat-buat, karena setiap perkara baru yang dibuat-buat adalah bid'ah, dan setiap bid'ah adalah kesesatan.'

2853.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Zuhri that he said: 'Those of our scholars who came before us used to say: Clinging to the Sunnah is salvation. Knowledge is being lifted rapidly — its preservation is the stability of religion and worldly affairs. And in knowledge's departure is the harm of both religion and worldly affairs.'
2853.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Zuhri bahwa ia berkata: 'Para ulama kami yang terdahulu biasa berkata: Berpegang teguh pada Sunnah adalah keselamatan. Ilmu diangkat dengan cepat — memeliharanya adalah stabilitas agama dan urusan dunia. Dan dalam hilangnya ilmu terdapat bahaya bagi agama dan urusan dunia.'

2854.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn al-Daylami that he said: 'I have been informed that the first thing that goes away from religion is abandoning the Sunnah. Religion goes away one Sunnah at a time, just as a rope loses its strength one strand at a time.'
2854.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn al-Dailami bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku diberitahu bahwa hal pertama yang hilang dari agama adalah meninggalkan Sunnah. Agama pergi satu Sunnah demi satu Sunnah, seperti tali kehilangan kekuatannya satu helai demi satu helai.'

2855.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hassan (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'No people introduce a religious innovation except that Allah removes from their Sunnah something equivalent to it — and He does not restore it to them until the Day of Resurrection.'
2855.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hassan (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Tidak ada kaum yang memperkenalkan bid'ah dalam agama mereka kecuali Allah mengambil dari Sunnah mereka sesuatu yang setara dengannya — dan Dia tidak mengembalikannya kepada mereka sampai Hari Kiamat.'

2856.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qilabah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'No man introduces a religious innovation except that he makes permissible the sword.'
2856.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qilabah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Tidak ada seorang pun yang memperkenalkan bid'ah dalam agama kecuali ia telah menghalalkan pedang.'

2857.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qilabah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The people of deviant desires are the people of misguidance, and I see their end as nothing but the Fire. Test them — there is not one among them who adopts an opinion or narrates a hadith except that he turns it around until he makes it fit his desire.'
2857.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qilabah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kaum pengikut hawa nafsu adalah kaum yang sesat, dan aku tidak melihat tujuan akhir mereka selain neraka. Uji mereka — tidak ada seorangpun di antara mereka yang mengambil pendapat atau meriwayatkan hadits kecuali ia memutar-balikkannya hingga membuatnya sesuai dengan keinginannya.'

2858.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Mas'ud and Hudhayfah (may Allah be pleased with them both) that they were sitting together when a man came and asked them about something. Ibn Mas'ud said to Hudhayfah: 'Why do you think they brought questions to us about things that Allah has not yet decreed? Indeed if it comes to pass, we will answer it, but if it does not come to pass, we will have prepared against something that has not occurred.'
2858.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Mas'ud dan Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa keduanya sedang duduk bersama ketika seorang pria datang dan bertanya kepada mereka tentang sesuatu. Ibnu Mas'ud berkata kepada Hudzaifah: 'Mengapa menurutmu mereka membawa pertanyaan kepada kami tentang hal-hal yang belum ditetapkan Allah? Sesungguhnya jika itu terjadi, kami akan menjawabnya, tetapi jika tidak terjadi, kami akan mempersiapkan diri menghadapi sesuatu yang belum terjadi.'

2859.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Nazzal ibn Sabrah that he said: 'I never missed a sermon that Abdullah ibn Mas'ud delivered in Kufa. One day I heard him, and he was asked about a man who had divorced his wife eight times. He said: You intend to harm her so that you can take away what you gave her — she is forbidden to him three times and she has what remains.'
2859.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Nazzal ibn Sabrah bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku tidak pernah melewatkan satu pun khutbah yang disampaikan Abdullah ibn Mas'ud di Kufah. Suatu hari aku mendengarnya, dan ia ditanya tentang seorang pria yang telah menceraikan istrinya delapan kali. Ia berkata: Kalian bermaksud menzaliminya sehingga kalian dapat mengambil kembali apa yang kalian berikan kepadanya — ia haram baginya tiga kali dan ia berhak atas apa yang tersisa.'

2860.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Nazzal ibn Sabrah that he said: 'I was present with Abdullah ibn Mas'ud when a man and a woman came to him regarding a forbidden declaration. He said: Allah has clarified — whoever commits what Allah has forbidden is subject to the ruling of Allah. Then he passed judgment between them.'
2860.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Nazzal ibn Sabrah bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku hadir bersama Abdullah ibn Mas'ud ketika seorang pria dan wanita datang kepadanya mengenai pernyataan pengharaman. Ia berkata: Allah telah menjelaskan — siapapun yang melakukan apa yang Allah haramkan tunduk pada hukum Allah. Kemudian ia memutuskan perkara di antara keduanya.'

2861.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Sirin (may Allah be pleased with him) that he would not give his own personal opinion on anything unless he had heard a transmitted report about it.
2861.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Sirin (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia tidak akan memberikan pendapat pribadinya tentang sesuatu kecuali jika ia telah mendengar riwayat yang diriwayatkan tentangnya.

2862.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-A'mash (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I never heard Ibrahim al-Nakha'i give his personal opinion on anything at all.'
2862.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-A'masy (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku tidak pernah mendengar Ibrahim al-Nakha'i memberikan pendapat pribadinya tentang sesuatu sama sekali.'

2863.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I have not expressed a personal opinion for thirty years.' Abu Hilal said: 'For forty years.'
2863.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku tidak mengungkapkan pendapat pribadi selama tiga puluh tahun.' Abu Hilal berkata: 'Selama empat puluh tahun.'

2864.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abd al-Aziz ibn Rufay' that he said: 'Ata' was asked about something and said: I do not know. It was said to him: Will you not give your personal opinion about it? He said: I am ashamed before Allah that anyone should find in me a deficiency in my religion.'
2864.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abd al-Aziz ibn Rufai' bahwa ia berkata: 'Atha' ditanya tentang sesuatu dan berkata: Aku tidak tahu. Dikatakan kepadanya: Tidakkah kamu akan memberikan pendapat pribadimu tentang itu? Ia berkata: Aku malu kepada Allah jika seseorang menemukan kekurangan dalam agamaku.'

2865.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Sha'bi (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man came and asked him about something. He said: Ibn Mas'ud used to say such and such about it. The man said: Tell me your own opinion. He said: Are you not amazed at this man who asks me for my personal opinion? I am telling you what Ibn Mas'ud said and you ask me for my opinion?
2865.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Sya'bi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang pria datang dan bertanya kepadanya tentang sesuatu. Ia berkata: Ibnu Mas'ud biasa berkata begini dan begitu tentang itu. Pria itu berkata: Beritahuku pendapatmu sendiri. Ia berkata: Tidakkah kamu heran dengan pria ini yang memintaku pendapat pribadinya? Aku memberitahumu apa yang dikatakan Ibnu Mas'ud dan kamu meminta pendapatku?

2866.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Sha'bi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Beware of analogical reasoning. By the One in Whose hand is my soul — if you resort to analogical reasoning you will certainly make the forbidden lawful and make the lawful forbidden. But whatever reaches you from those who came before the people of innovation — hold fast to it.'
2866.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Sya'bi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Waspadalah terhadap qiyas (penalaran analogis). Demi Dzat yang jiwaku ada di tangan-Nya — jika kalian menggunakan qiyas, kalian pasti akan menghalalkan yang haram dan mengharamkan yang halal. Tetapi apapun yang sampai kepada kalian dari mereka yang datang sebelum kaum pembuat bid'ah — berpeganglah teguh padanya.'

2867.iwh_en: It was narrated from Alqamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man came to Abdullah ibn Mas'ud and said: He has divorced his wife eight times last night. He said: In one statement? He said: Yes, in one statement. He said: They want to allow him to make it worse for her and take back what he gave her. She is forbidden to him with one pronouncement and she retains what remains of her rights.
2867.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Alqamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang pria datang kepada Abdullah ibn Mas'ud dan berkata: Ia telah menceraikan istrinya delapan kali semalam. Ia berkata: Dalam satu pernyataan? Ia berkata: Ya, dalam satu pernyataan. Ia berkata: Mereka ingin mengizinkannya memperburuk keadaannya dan mengambil kembali apa yang ia berikan. Ia haram baginya dengan satu pengucapan dan ia mempertahankan apa yang tersisa dari haknya.

2868.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Qasim (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'It is better for a man to live in ignorance — after he knows what Allah requires of him — than to say what he does not know.'
2868.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Qasim (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Lebih baik bagi seseorang untuk hidup dalam ketidaktahuan — setelah ia mengetahui apa yang Allah wajibkan atasnya — daripada mengatakan apa yang tidak ia ketahui.'

2869.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Qasim (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was asked and said: 'By Allah, we do not know everything you ask about. If we did know, we would not hide it from you, and it would not be lawful for us to hide it from you.'
2869.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Qasim (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia ditanya dan berkata: 'Demi Allah, kami tidak mengetahui semua yang kalian tanyakan. Jika kami tahu, kami tidak akan menyembunyikannya dari kalian, dan tidak halal bagi kami untuk menyembunyikannya dari kalian.'

2870.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Qasim (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was asked about something and said: 'I have no need to consult anyone about it, and I have nothing to do with this matter.'
2870.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Qasim (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia ditanya tentang sesuatu dan berkata: 'Aku tidak perlu berkonsultasi dengan siapapun tentang itu, dan aku tidak ada urusan dengan perkara ini.'

2871.iwh_en: It was narrated from Yahya (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said to al-Qasim: 'It is difficult for me that you are asked about something you do not know, when your father was an imam.' Al-Qasim said: 'What is more difficult than that in the sight of Allah and in the eyes of those who understand what pleases Allah is for a scholar to speak without knowledge. My father — by Allah — never said anything unless he had a text for it from the Book of Allah or the Sunnah of the Prophet ﷺ.'
2871.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Yahya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata kepada al-Qasim: 'Sulit bagiku bahwa kamu ditanya tentang sesuatu yang tidak kamu ketahui, padahal ayahmu adalah seorang imam.' Al-Qasim berkata: 'Yang lebih sulit dari itu di sisi Allah dan di mata mereka yang memahami apa yang menyenangkan Allah adalah seorang ulama berbicara tanpa ilmu. Ayahku — demi Allah — tidak pernah mengatakan sesuatu kecuali ia memiliki nash dari Kitabullah atau Sunnah Nabi ﷺ.'

2872.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Musayyib ibn Rafi' (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'When they were confronted with a matter in which there was no transmitted report from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, they would gather together and deliberate. Whatever they agreed upon — that is the truth. The truth is in what they saw, and what they saw is in agreement.'
2872.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Musayyib ibn Rafi' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika mereka dihadapkan dengan suatu perkara yang tidak ada riwayat dari Rasulullah ﷺ tentangnya, mereka berkumpul dan bermusyawarah. Apapun yang mereka sepakati — itulah kebenaran. Kebenaran ada pada apa yang mereka lihat, dan apa yang mereka lihat ada dalam kesepakatan.'

2873.iwh_en: It was narrated from Wahb ibn Amr al-Jumahi (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Do not be hasty with a trial before it descends — for indeed if it descends, those with sound understanding and discernment will know what to do with it. But if you rush to it before it comes, there is a risk you will stumble.'
2873.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Wahb ibn Amr al-Jumahi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Jangan terburu-buru menghadapi ujian sebelum ia turun — karena sesungguhnya jika ia turun, mereka yang memiliki pemahaman dan kecerdasan yang baik akan tahu apa yang harus dilakukan dengannya. Tetapi jika kalian terburu-buru menghadapinya sebelum datang, ada risiko kalian akan tersandung.'

2874.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Salamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ was asked about a matter that had arisen for which there was nothing in the Book or the Sunnah. He said: 'The devout believers should look into it.'
2874.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Salamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ ditanya tentang suatu perkara yang muncul yang tidak ada dalam Kitab maupun Sunnah. Beliau bersabda: 'Para mukmin yang saleh hendaklah melihat ke dalamnya.'

2875.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Qasim (may Allah be pleased with him) through Ibn Awn that he said: 'You ask about things we would never ask about, and you probe into things we would never probe into, and you ask about things I do not know what they are.'
2875.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Qasim (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui Ibnu Awn bahwa ia berkata: 'Kalian bertanya tentang hal-hal yang tidak pernah kami tanyakan, dan kalian menyelidiki hal-hal yang tidak pernah kami selidiki, dan kalian bertanya tentang hal-hal yang aku tidak tahu apa itu.'

2876.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'There will come people who will dispute with you using ambiguities from the Quran — so refute them with the Sunnah. For indeed the people of the Sunnah are the most knowledgeable of the Book of Allah.'
2876.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umar ibn al-Khattab (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Akan datang orang-orang yang akan berdebat dengan kalian menggunakan ayat-ayat samar dari Al-Quran — maka bantahlah mereka dengan Sunnah. Karena sesungguhnya orang-orang Sunnah adalah yang paling berpengetahuan tentang Kitabullah.'

2877.iwh_en: It was narrated from Urwah ibn al-Zubayr (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The affairs of the Children of Israel remained balanced with nothing wrong in them, until their born-in-Israel successors emerged who gave their personal opinions in matters of religion. Then their affairs became disarrayed, and they led both themselves and those who followed them astray.'
2877.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Urwah ibn al-Zubair (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Urusan Bani Israel tetap seimbang tanpa ada yang salah di dalamnya, hingga penerus mereka yang lahir di Israel muncul yang memberikan pendapat pribadi mereka dalam masalah agama. Kemudian urusan mereka menjadi kacau, dan mereka menyesatkan diri sendiri dan orang-orang yang mengikuti mereka.'

2878.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that a man came to him and asked him about something. Ibn Umar said to him: 'Do not ask about what has not yet happened — for I heard Umar ibn al-Khattab curse whoever asked about what had not yet come to be.'
2878.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa seorang pria datang kepadanya dan menanyakan sesuatu. Ibnu Umar berkata kepadanya: 'Jangan bertanya tentang apa yang belum terjadi — karena aku mendengar Umar ibn al-Khattab mengutuk siapapun yang bertanya tentang apa yang belum terjadi.'

2879.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Zuhri (may Allah be pleased with him) that it reached him that Zayd ibn Thabit al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) used to say, when asked about a matter: 'Has this actually occurred?' If they said yes, it has occurred, he would narrate about it what he knew. If they said no, he would say: 'Leave it until it occurs.'
2879.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Zuhri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa telah sampai kepadanya bahwa Zaid ibn Tsabit al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) biasa berkata, ketika ditanya tentang suatu perkara: 'Apakah ini benar-benar telah terjadi?' Jika mereka berkata ya, itu telah terjadi, ia akan menceritakan tentangnya apa yang ia ketahui. Jika mereka berkata tidak, ia akan berkata: 'Tinggalkan itu sampai terjadi.'

2880.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ammar ibn Yasir (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was asked about a matter and said: 'Has this occurred yet?' They said: No. He said: 'Leave us until it occurs, and when it occurs we will give you our verdict, Allah willing.'
2880.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ammar ibn Yasir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia ditanya tentang suatu perkara dan berkata: 'Apakah ini sudah terjadi?' Mereka berkata: Tidak. Ia berkata: 'Biarkan kami sampai itu terjadi, dan ketika terjadi kami akan memberikan putusan kami, insya Allah.'

2881.iwh_en: It was narrated from Tawus (may Allah be pleased with him) that Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) said from the pulpit: 'I warn in the name of Allah any man who asks about what has not yet occurred — for indeed Allah has already explained what is going to be.'
2881.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Thawus (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata dari atas mimbar: 'Aku memperingatkan demi Allah setiap orang yang bertanya tentang apa yang belum terjadi — karena sesungguhnya Allah telah menjelaskan apa yang akan terjadi.'

2882.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that he said: 'I have not seen a people better than the companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. They only asked him about thirteen matters in the entire period from his migration to his death — all of which are in the Quran. They were only concerned with matters that actually affected them.'
2882.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku tidak pernah melihat kaum yang lebih baik dari para sahabat Rasulullah ﷺ. Mereka hanya bertanya kepada beliau tentang tiga belas perkara sepanjang masa dari hijrahnya hingga kematiannya — semuanya ada dalam Al-Quran. Mereka hanya memperhatikan perkara-perkara yang benar-benar mempengaruhi mereka.'

2883.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umayr ibn Ishaq (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I have met more companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ than those who preceded me from among them. I have not seen a people with a more relaxed manner of living or with less harshness than them.'
2883.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umayr ibn Ishaq (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku telah bertemu lebih banyak sahabat Rasulullah ﷺ daripada mereka yang mendahuluiku dari kalangan mereka. Aku tidak pernah melihat kaum yang lebih santai cara hidupnya atau lebih sedikit kerasnya dari mereka.'

2884.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ubadah ibn Nusayy al-Kindi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was asked about a woman who died with a group of people with no male guardian present. He said: 'I have known people who did not impose the strictness that people impose today. They would bury whomever died and did what they could.'
2884.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ubadah ibn Nusay al-Kindi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia ditanya tentang seorang wanita yang meninggal bersama sekelompok orang tanpa wali laki-laki yang hadir. Ia berkata: 'Aku mengenal orang-orang yang tidak memaksakan ketatnya hukum seperti yang orang-orang terapkan hari ini. Mereka mengubur siapapun yang meninggal dan melakukan apa yang mereka bisa.'

2885.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hisham ibn Muslim al-Qurayshi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I was with Ibn Muhayriz at Marj al-Diybaj and I noticed a solitary moment of his. I asked him about it and he said: I am reflecting upon the fact that the scholars are departing and the ignorant are becoming plentiful, and that the people are clinging to their desires, and that I see this generation after me wearing the garment of religion and removing it whenever they wish.'
2885.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hisyam ibn Muslim al-Qurasyi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku bersama Ibnu Muhairiz di Marj al-Diybaj dan aku melihat saat-saat menyendirinya. Aku bertanya kepadanya tentang itu dan ia berkata: Aku sedang merenungkan fakta bahwa para ulama sedang pergi dan orang-orang bodoh semakin banyak, dan bahwa orang-orang berpegang pada hawa nafsu mereka, dan bahwa aku melihat generasi setelahku mengenakan jubah agama dan melepaskannya kapanpun mereka mau.'

2886.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Sha'bi (may Allah be pleased with him) that Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'O people, we do not know — perhaps we order you to do things that are not lawful for you, and perhaps we forbid you things that are lawful for you.'
2886.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Sya'bi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata: 'Wahai manusia, kami tidak tahu — mungkin kami memerintahkan kalian melakukan hal-hal yang tidak halal bagi kalian, dan mungkin kami melarang kalian dari hal-hal yang halal bagi kalian.'

2887.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Idris (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I came out from the presence of Ibrahim al-Nakha'i and encountered Hammad. He loaded me with eight chapters of questions. I asked him about them and he answered four and left four.'
2887.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Idris (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku keluar dari hadapan Ibrahim al-Nakha'i dan bertemu dengan Hammad. Ia membebani aku dengan delapan bab pertanyaan. Aku bertanya kepadanya dan ia menjawab empat dan meninggalkan empat.'

2888.iwh_en: It was narrated from Zubayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I never asked Ibrahim al-Nakha'i about anything without seeing discomfort on his face.'
2888.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Zubaid (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku tidak pernah bertanya kepada Ibrahim al-Nakha'i tentang sesuatu tanpa melihat ketidaknyamanan di wajahnya.'

2889.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umar ibn Abi Za'idah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I have never seen anyone who said more often — when asked about something — "I have no knowledge of this" than al-Sha'bi.'
2889.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umar ibn Abi Za'idah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku tidak pernah melihat siapapun yang lebih sering berkata — ketika ditanya tentang sesuatu — "Aku tidak memiliki pengetahuan tentang ini" daripada al-Sya'bi.'

2890.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Awn (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Al-Sha'bi, whenever something came to him, he was cautious. As for Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, he would give opinions and give more opinions.' Abu Asim said: 'In this regard, al-Sha'bi was in a better state in the eyes of Ibn Awn than Ibrahim.'
2890.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Awn (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Al-Sya'bi, kapanpun sesuatu datang kepadanya, ia berhati-hati. Adapun Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, ia akan memberikan pendapat dan terus memberikan pendapat.' Abu Asim berkata: 'Dalam hal ini, al-Sya'bi berada dalam keadaan yang lebih baik di mata Ibnu Awn daripada Ibrahim.'

2891.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ja'far ibn Iyas (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I said to Sa'id ibn Jubayr: Why do you not give any rulings on divorce?' He said: 'There is no aspect of it that I have not asked about — but I dislike issuing rulings about it because the scholars differ greatly about it.'
2891.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ja'far ibn Iyas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku berkata kepada Sa'id ibn Jubair: Mengapa kamu tidak memberikan fatwa tentang talak?' Ia berkata: 'Tidak ada aspek darinya yang tidak aku tanyakan — tetapi aku tidak suka mengeluarkan fatwa tentangnya karena para ulama sangat berbeda pendapat tentangnya.'

2892.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Layla (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I met in this mosque one hundred and twenty of the Ansar. Not one of them narrated a hadith without wishing that a brother of his would spare him from narrating it — and not one of them was asked for a religious verdict without wishing that a brother of his would spare him from giving it.'
2892.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Layla (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku bertemu di masjid ini seratus dua puluh orang dari Anshar. Tidak ada seorang pun dari mereka yang meriwayatkan hadits tanpa berharap seorang saudaranya akan meringankannya dari meriwayatkannya — dan tidak ada seorang pun dari mereka yang diminta fatwa agama tanpa berharap seorang saudaranya akan meringankannya dari memberikannya.'

2893.iwh_en: It was narrated from Dawud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I asked al-Sha'bi: How did you used to handle it when asked questions?' He said: 'You have found the right person to ask. When a man was asked, he would say to his companion: Give them the ruling. And so it would go back and forth until it returned to the first man who was asked — and he would give the ruling.'
2893.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Dawud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku bertanya kepada al-Sya'bi: Bagaimana kalian biasa menanganinya ketika ditanya pertanyaan?' Ia berkata: 'Kamu telah menemukan orang yang tepat untuk ditanya. Ketika seseorang ditanya, ia akan berkata kepada temannya: Berikan mereka fatwanya. Dan begitulah seterusnya bolak-balik sampai kembali ke orang pertama yang ditanya — dan ia pun memberikan fatwa.'

2894.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn al-Munkadir (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'A scholar enters into what lies between Allah and His servants — so let him seek a way out for himself.'
2894.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu al-Munkadir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Seorang ulama memasuki apa yang terletak antara Allah dan hamba-hamba-Nya — maka hendaklah ia mencari jalan keluar untuk dirinya sendiri.'

2895.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ma'n ibn Abd al-Rahman (may Allah be pleased with him) that he showed a document to Mis'ar and swore by Allah that it was in his father's handwriting, in which was written: Abdullah ibn Mas'ud said: 'By the One besides Whom there is no deity — I have not seen anyone more knowledgeable of what Allah has sent down than myself. But I am not the best of those to whom it was revealed.'
2895.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ma'n ibn Abd al-Rahman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia menunjukkan sebuah dokumen kepada Mis'ar dan bersumpah demi Allah bahwa itu adalah tulisan tangan ayahnya, di dalamnya tertulis: Abdullah ibn Mas'ud berkata: 'Demi Dzat yang tiada ilah selain-Nya — aku tidak pernah melihat siapapun yang lebih berpengetahuan tentang apa yang Allah turunkan daripada diriku sendiri. Namun aku bukanlah yang terbaik dari mereka yang kepadanya wahyu itu diturunkan.'

2896.iwh_en: It was narrated from Uthman ibn Hadir al-Azdi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I entered upon Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) and said: Give me advice. He said: Yes — hold fast to taqwa (fear of Allah) and uprightness, follow (the transmitted knowledge) and do not innovate.'
2896.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Utsman ibn Hadir al-Azdi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku masuk menemui Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) dan berkata: Berikan aku nasihat. Ia berkata: Ya — berpegang teguhlah pada taqwa kepada Allah dan istiqamah, ikutilah (ilmu yang diriwayatkan) dan jangan berinovasi.'

2897.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Sirin (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'They used to consider that one was on the right path so long as one was upon the transmitted narrations.'
2897.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Sirin (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Mereka biasa beranggapan bahwa seseorang berada di jalan yang benar selama ia berpegang pada riwayat-riwayat yang diriwayatkan.'
